description: Controlled vocabulary for paleo-relative sea-level indicators, qualifiers, and measurement semantics. Downstream records must map to these controlled terms.

sea_level_indicator.definition: A sample point with an interpretable indicative meaning relative to sea level.
diagnostic_criteria: Indicator type has a defensible modern analog or geomorphic meaning relative to sea level.
Elevation or depth can be associated with the indicator horizon or landform.
Interpretation is not purely terrestrial or marine limiting unless explicitly encoded as such.

marine_limiting.definition: A sample point indicating sea level was at or above a minimum marine threshold, without a narrow indicative meaning.
diagnostic_criteria: Marine influence is clear.
Record constrains only one side of sea-level position.
Indicative range is not sufficiently narrow for an index point.

terrestrial_limiting.definition: A sample point indicating sea level was at or below a maximum terrestrial threshold, without a narrow indicative meaning.
diagnostic_criteria: Terrestrial setting is clear.
Record constrains only one side of sea-level position.
Indicative range is not sufficiently narrow for an index point.

in_situ.definition: Material or landform preserved in original depositional or growth context.

reworked.definition: Material transported or redeposited after original formation.

dated.definition: Record has at least one explicit age constraint.

undated.definition: Record lacks a direct or associated age constraint.

growth_position.definition: Biogenic material is preserved in life position or growth position.

ex_situ.definition: Material is not preserved in original position and requires interpretive caution.

anthropogenically_disturbed.definition: Record has likely been modified by excavation, fill, quarrying, or other human disturbance.

uncertain_interpretation.definition: Marine versus terrestrial meaning, facies interpretation, or sea-level significance remains ambiguous.

elevation_above_msl.definition: Elevation reported or derived relative to mean sea level.

depth_below_msl.definition: Depth reported or derived below mean sea level.

indicative_range.definition: Range of vertical occurrence of the modern analog or interpreted sea-level meaning.

upper_lower_elevation_bounds.definition: Measured upper and lower limits bounding the indicator horizon or landform.

reported_rsl.definition: Paleo relative sea level reported directly by the source.

derived_rsl.definition: Paleo relative sea level derived during normalization from elevation and indicative meaning.

dating_methods.other: Other dating or age attribution method

reported.definition: Coordinates explicitly reported in the source.

inferred_text.definition: Coordinates inferred from explicit place names or textual descriptions.

inferred_map.definition: Coordinates inferred from map or figure interpretation.

depth_sources.other: Derived from another bathymetry or topography source

raised_beach.definition: Emergent beach deposit or ridge preserving a former shoreline position above present sea level.
diagnostic_criteria: Beach gravel, sand, or ridge morphology with shoreline organization.
Sedimentary structures or landform geometry consistent with wave action.
Stratigraphic or geomorphic context ties the deposit to former coastal conditions.

submerged_beach.definition: Submerged beach deposit or ridge below present sea level representing a former shoreline.
diagnostic_criteria: Submerged beach morphology or sedimentology is documented.
Marine context is explicit.
Depth or elevation can be tied to the feature.

beachrock.definition: Cemented beach sediment formed within the intertidal to supratidal coastal zone.
diagnostic_criteria: Lithified beach deposit with shoreline-parallel geometry or beach facies.
Marine cementation context is reported or inferred.
Elevation can be associated with the cemented horizon.

coral_reef.definition: Reef framework or reef terrace preserving coral growth within a known water-depth envelope.
diagnostic_criteria: Coral reef facies or reef framework is described.
Ecological or taxonomic context supports marine depth interpretation.
Elevation/depth can be associated with the reef surface or dated horizon.

coral_head.definition: Individual coral colony or coral head used as a paleo sea-level indicator.
diagnostic_criteria: Individual coral sample is identifiable.
Growth position or ecological depth range is reported or inferable.
Age can be tied to the sampled colony or associated analysis.

marine_terrace.definition: Wave-cut or wave-built terrace formed by prolonged shoreline occupation.
diagnostic_criteria: Terrace morphology and coastal origin are described.
Terrace tread, shoreline angle, or correlated deposit is measured.
Marine genesis is explicit and not purely tectonic or fluvial.

guyot_or_drowned_platform.definition: Flat-topped seamount, tablemount, drowned volcanic island, atoll, or drowned shallow-water carbonate platform whose summit or platform depth records former near-sea-level planation, carbonate growth, emergence, drowning, or subsidence history.
diagnostic_criteria: Feature is explicitly described as a guyot, tablemount, flat-topped seamount, drowned island, atoll, or drowned carbonate platform.
Summit, plateau, platform, break, or top depth/elevation is reported or can be tied to a named feature.
Text links the feature to shallow-water carbonate growth, wave/base-level erosion, emergence, drowning, subsidence, or sea-level history.

tidal_notch.definition: Erosional notch or bioerosional notch formed in a narrow vertical range around sea level.
diagnostic_criteria: Notch morphology is documented.
Sea-level linkage is explicit through modern analog or process interpretation.
Measurement references the notch apex, retreat point, or comparable horizon.

lagoonal_sediment.definition: Lagoonal facies indicating a protected shallow-water environment tied to relative sea level.
diagnostic_criteria: Lagoonal lithofacies, fauna, flora, or geochemistry are described.
Environmental interpretation is marine to brackish and coastal.
Vertical position of the lagoonal horizon is constrained.

estuarine_sediment.definition: Estuarine facies preserving tidal or brackish-water conditions relevant to sea-level position.
diagnostic_criteria: Estuarine sedimentology or paleoecology is identified.
Marine influence is explicit.
Record can be located within a vertical coastal framework.

deltaic_sequence.definition: Deltaic facies succession whose marine-terrestrial transition constrains relative sea level.
diagnostic_criteria: Deltaic depositional environment is explicit.
Vertical facies transition relevant to shoreline position is described.
Point-level sample or horizon can be isolated from site-level interpretation.

saltmarsh_peat_or_mud.definition: Saltmarsh peat or mud used as a sea-level indicator through tidal-elevation ecology.
diagnostic_criteria: Saltmarsh setting is supported by sediment, macrofossils, microfossils, or geochemistry.
Tidal-elevation relation is documented or inferable.
Horizon elevation is tied to a sample point or core depth.

mangrove_peat_or_mud.definition: Mangrove peat or mud indicating intertidal to supratidal coastal conditions.
diagnostic_criteria: Mangrove botanical or sedimentological evidence is present.
Coastal tidal setting is explicit.
Vertical position is constrained by elevation or core depth.

tidal_flat_sediment.definition: Intertidal flat sediment or facies indicating a constrained tidal elevation range.
diagnostic_criteria: Tidal-flat environment is explicit from facies or biota.
Marine-tidal influence is clear.
Sample horizon can be related to an intertidal vertical zone.

shore_platform.definition: Shore platform or wave-cut bench formed near sea level.
diagnostic_criteria: Planar erosional platform geometry is described.
Marine wave-cut origin is explicit.
Measurement references tread or shoreline angle with stated assumptions.

wave_cut_notch_or_bench.definition: Wave-cut notch or small bench linked to shoreline processes.
diagnostic_criteria: Erosional morphology is coastal and wave-related.
Vertical reference horizon is defined.
Interpretation distinguishes it from non-marine weathering features.

marine_shell_bed.definition: Marine shell-bearing deposit used to indicate marine inundation or shoreline position.
diagnostic_criteria: Marine shells are explicitly described.
Depositional context and reworking state are considered.
Vertical and stratigraphic position are constrained.

intertidal_bioconstruction.definition: Biogenic coastal construction such as vermetids, oysters, or algal rims formed within a narrow tidal range.
diagnostic_criteria: Bioconstruction type is identified.
Modern ecological vertical range is known or inferable.
Measurement references the biogenic growth horizon.

speleothem_sea_level_proxy.definition: Coastal cave speleothem, phreatic overgrowth, or related proxy used to constrain relative sea level.
diagnostic_criteria: Cave coastal setting is explicit.
Proxy type has recognized sea-level significance.
Elevation and age can be tied to the sampled feature.

subtidal_facies.definition: Subtidal marine facies constraining relative sea level through water-depth interpretation.
diagnostic_criteria: Subtidal depositional environment is described.
Water-depth interpretation is explicit or defensible.
Sample horizon can be isolated as a point-level record.

terrestrial_over_marine_contact.definition: Contact where terrestrial deposits overlie marine deposits, constraining relative sea-level fall or emergence.
diagnostic_criteria: Contact is stratigraphically explicit.
Marine and terrestrial facies are both supported.
Point-level elevation or depth references the contact.

marine_over_terrestrial_contact.definition: Contact where marine deposits overlie terrestrial deposits, constraining transgression or marine inundation.
diagnostic_criteria: Contact is stratigraphically explicit.
Marine and terrestrial facies are both supported.
Point-level elevation or depth references the contact.
